title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Friends

lines.text: A Video Store, Monica is running in to return a video.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to clerk) Hi.
The clerk enters the video into the computer.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Six dollars, please.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Six? I just had it for one night. It's three.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Eight o'clock is the cut-off and, (looks at his watch) aww, it's 8:02.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know in a weird way, you have too much power. Look, you're gonna have to help me out here, 'cause I only have three.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: (standing behind her, without his moustache) I can help with that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God. Richard? (turns around) Hi!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! Your lip went bald. (Richard pays the clerk) Hey, thanks.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: So, you look great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: No you do. You... just...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You've got panties stuck to your leg.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (removes them) That's because I-I was just grabbing some things out of the dryer, and it's static cling. Or maybe it's just that God knew I'd be running into you and saw an opportunity.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: It's good to see you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's good to see you too.
They hug, and Richard notices a stocking stuck to Monica's back, which he removes for her.
Opening Credits
continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You see that guy? He's in classics now, but y'know as soon as we leave he's going straight to the porn.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: He's gonna go up to the counter with Citizen Kane, Vertigo, and Clockwork Orgy. (they both laugh) This is nice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: I missed this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: So, you wanna get a hamburger or something?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, um, I don't know if that's a good idea.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Oh. Look, just friends, I won't grope you. I promise.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I just I think that it's too soon.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: No it's not too soon, I had lunch at a eleven.
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler, Ross. Joey, and Rachel are eating breakfast. Chandler is holding a bottle of Hersey's Syrup.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, baby!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Making chocolate milk. Do you want some?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No thanks. I'm 29.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at her watch) Oh my God, I gotta go to work!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh sweetie, when do you think you're going to get off tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I don't know honey. It's gonna be really late.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh come on, not again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I'm sorry. Look, I'll make a deal with you all right? Okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: For every night that you're asleep before I get home from work...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I will wake you up in a way that's proved very popular in the past.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, if you need to stay late, I want to be supportive of that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering, holding a pair of Roller Blades) Hi.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Look at you. Since when do you roller blade?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Since tomorrow. I met this really cute guy in the park and he like y'know, jogs, and blades, and swims, and so y'know we made a deal that's he's going to teach me all sorts of jock stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And what are you going to do for him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm going to let him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cool.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her bedroom) Morning.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Somebody got in late last night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah well, I ran into Richard.
They all gasp

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When did this happen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, um, around 8:02. We ah, talked for a little while, and then um, we went out for an innocent burger.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, there's no such thing as an innocent burger.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, are you gonna see him again?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she spent the last six months getting over him, and now she's celebrating that by going on a date with him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not a date, okay. I'm just gonna teach him how to make lasagne for some pot luck dinner he has.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you might wanna make a little extra, y'know you'll probably be hungry after the sex.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're not gonna have sex! Okay, nothing's changed here. He still doesn't want children and I still do, so that's why we're just gonna be friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Naked friends.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is watching TV.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you have any ice?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check the freezer. If there's none in there, then we're probably out. Are you just getting in from work? It's late.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know. I had the greatest day though, I got to sit in on the meeting with the reps from Calvin Klien. I told my boss I liked this line of lingerie, she ordered a ton of it. How was your day?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I discovered I'm able to count all of my teeth using just my tongue.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm. (she opens the freezer) Umm, why do you have a copy of The Shining in your freezer?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I was reading it last night, and I got scared, so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But ah, you're safe from it if it's in the freezer?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, safer. Y'know, I mean I never start reading The Shining, without making sure we've got plenty of room in the freezer, y'know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How often do you read it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Haven't you ever read the same book over and over again?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, umm, I guess I read Little Women more than once. But I mean that's a classic, what's so great about The Shining?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The question should be Rach, what is not so great about The Shining. Okay? And the answer would be: nothing. All right? This is like the scariest book ever. I bet it's way better than that classic of yours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Ah, well we'll just see about that, okay. I will read The Shining, (she tries to take the book away from him but he doesn't want to let it go) and you will read Little Women.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, you got it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, now Rach, these ah, these little women.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How little are they? I mean, are they like scary little?
Central Perk, Chandler and Ross are there, Phoebe is entering with her date Robert.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, Chandler, Ross, this is Robert.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hey.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Robert) You've have lipstick right here (points to her cheek). That's okay, it's mine, we just kissed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Robert) So ah, isn't a bit cold out for shorts?

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Well, I'm from California.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right, right. Sometimes you guys just burst into flames.
Robert leans back on the arm of the chair and allows Chandler to see up his shorts and sees 'little Robert.' Chandler is horrified by this view.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (standing up) I'm up! I'm up, I've gotten up now! Anybody ah, want anything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll have coffee.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Yeah, me too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, make that three.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay Ross, why don't you come with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (goes over to the counter) (to Chandler) What ah, what is the matter with you? What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Robert's coming out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, what do you mean, what? Is he gay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. He.....he's coming out of his shorts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The man is showing brain.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you sure? (Chandler nods: Yes!) Hold on. (walks over behind the couch) I'm sorry you guys, that was a coffee and a....

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: We could write it down for you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, that won't be ah, that won't be necessary (leans down and looks up Robert's shorts, seeing Robert's package.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) Wellll?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do we do? What do we do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I suppose we just try to not look directly at it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like an eclipse.
Ross nods his head.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is teaching Richard how to make lasagne.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: So when people complement me on my cooking should I, what do I say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You say 'Thank you very much,' and then you buy me something pretty. Come on, we're gonna put are hands in this bowl, and we're gonna start squishing the tomatoes.
They both start squishing the tomatoes.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Ew, this feels very weird.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You touch people's eyeballs every day and this feels weird.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Yeah, well, sure I touch them, but I spent years learning not to squish them. (Monica grabs his hand in the tomatoes.) That's my hand.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oops.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Gotta keep squishing.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Tomatoes are squishing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
Richard squishes a little too hard and some lands on his shirt.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Op.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, gosh, you got some on your shirt.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hold on a second, just put a little club soda on it (does so) and it should umm, be.....
She is rubbing his chest and her voice trails off into silence, a long pause follows.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, you've got some on your pants.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: I'll just throw them out.
Central Perk, Chandler is watching Joey read Little Women, Ross is also there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: These little women. Wow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your liking it, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah! Amy just burned Jo's manuscript. I don't see how he could ever forgive her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, Jo's a girl, it's short for Josephine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But Jo's got a crush on Laurie. (Ross nods his head) Oh. You mean it's like a girl-girl thing? 'Cause that is the one thing missing from The Shining.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, actually Laurie's a boy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No wonder Rachel had to read this so many times.
Enter Phoebe and Robert

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! How'd the ah, basketball go?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, I learned how to shoot a lay-up, a foul shot, and a twenty-three pointer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean a three pointer?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I get more because I'm dainty.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: So um, is there a phone here, I can check my messages?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, in the back. You want a quarter?

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Oh, no thanks. I always carry one in my sock.
He puts his leg up on the couch to get the quarter, once again exposing himself to Chandler and Ross. In horror, Chandler, slides over and leans against Joey on the couch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (noticing Chandler) What are you doing? (he pushes Chandler back to his side of the couch) Get back over on your side of the... (sees Robert in all his glory) Hello!! (to Robert) Hi, I'm Joey, we haven't met.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Ah, good to meet you. Robert.
Robert walks away and the guys all start laughing in front of Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? (the guys keep laughing.) What? You guys, what is going on? You not like Robert? (the guys keep laughing.) Why are you laughing?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Calm down. There's no reason to get testy.
The guys start laughing harder.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys!! Come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry, it just seems that Robert isn't as concealed in the shorts area, as ah, one may have hoped.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Hey. (sits down)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Robert, could you ah, ha, pass me those cookies?

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Sure.
He puts his leg up on the table to pass Joey the cookies, and Phoebe sees what the guys are laughing at, and gasps.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is talking to Richard on the phone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, how'd the lasagne go over? (listens) Really?! Good. So you owe me three pretty things. (listens) Yeah, I've been thinking a lot about you too. (listens) I know. It's hard this whole platonic thing. (listens) It's a word!
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I do think it's better this way. (listens) Yeah, we're being smart. (gets up to answer the door) (listens) Yes, I'm sure.(she opens the door and it's Richard)

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You really sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll call you back. (starts kissing him)
Commercial Break
Monica's bedroom, she's in bed with Richard.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So we can be friends who sleep together.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Absolutely, this will just be something we do, like racquetball.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sounds smart and healthy to me. So um, just out of curiosity, um, do you currently have any other racquetball buddies?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Just your dad. (pause) Although that's actually racquetball. You know I-I do have a blind date with my sister's neighbour next Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You want me to cancel it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! (pushes him) No!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause if you do that means you'd be cancelling it for me, and we're just friends.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Exactly. (pushes her back)
Central Perk, Robert is picking up Phoebe for a date.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Ooh! Don't sit down!

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: You ready to go to the batting cage?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. And, first here's a gift.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Oh! Wow! Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stretchy pants! Why, those are the greatest things in the world! If I were you I would wear them every day, every day!

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Jeez, thank you really that is so nice. But um, to be honest, I don't think I can wear these, they're so tight, I feel like I'm on display. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's all right, that's well, I figured.... (they start to leave as Joey enters.)

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: (to Joey) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (starts to laugh.) How's it going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Rach, how you doing with The Shining?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Danny just went into room 217.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oooh, the next part's the best, when that dead lady in the bathtub...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no, meh-nah-nah-nah, come on you're gonna ruin it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right I'll talk in code. (to Ross and Chandler) Remember when the kid sees those two blanks in the hallway?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hmmm, that's very cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, all blank, and no blank, make's blank a blank blank. Oh no-no-no, no, the end when Jack almost kills them all with that blank, but then at last second they get away. Aww!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey! I can't believe you just did that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe she cracked your code!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, okay, Laurie proposes to Jo, and she says no, even though she's still in love with him, and then he ends up marring Amy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Mine was by accident! All right, the boiler explodes and destroys the hotel, and kills the dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eh. Beth dies.
Joey recoils and gasps in horror.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Beth, Beth dies?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is that true? If I keep reading is Beth gonna die?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, Beth doesn't die, she doesn't die. Does she Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey's asking if you've just ruined the first book he's ever loved that didn't star Jack Nicholson?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. She doesn't die.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then why would you say that?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, I wanted to hurt you.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: (running in) Oh, there they are! I-I dropped my keys.
He bends over to pick them up, right in front of Rachel, who then gets a free peep show.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Oh my....

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Got 'em.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is reading The Shining, as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (screams and grabs a potato masher to defend herself) Sorry. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You would not believe my day! I had to work two shifts, and then to top it off, I lost one of my fake boobs, (opens her coat revealing a large burn mark over her left breast.) in a grill fire.
Rachel starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you smiling at?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, I was just thinking you're day could still pick up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, right.
She goes into her bedroom, and sees Richard who has covered the room in roses and has two glasses of wine and a rose between his lips.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love this friend thing!
Central Perk, Phoebe, Chandler, and Ross are there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen, Robert's gonna be here any second so, will one of you just tell him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please, right now, no, every time I see him it's like 'Is it on the lose?' 'Is it watching me?'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can't tell him, you can't go up to a guy you barely know and talk about his.... stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's right, even if it's to say something complementary. (He stops and thinks about what he just said.)

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: (to Phoebe) So are ready for the gym? They've got this new rock climbing wall, we can spot each other.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I can spot you from here.

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, listen Robert...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Chandler) Hey, don't we have to...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we got, um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I think you're really, really great...

lines.speaker: Robert
lines.text: Oh God! Here we go again. Why does this keep happening to me? (spreads his legs) Is it something I'm putting out there? Is this my fault? Or am I just nuts?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I-I-I-I don't know, I don't know what to say.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (cleaning up the table) (to Robert) Hey buddy, this is a family place, put the mouse back in the house.
Robert looks down and realises the problem.

Richard's bedroom, Monica has covered it in rose pedals and candles. We hear Richard come in to the apartment, and Monica frantically throws the rest of the pedals on the bed, and jumps onto the bed and puts a rose in her mouth, and bites a thorn.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: (outside the bedroom) Really?! Well, it's just like everyone else's apartment. It's got rooms, walls, and ceilings.

lines.speaker: Richard's Date
lines.text: Well, I just wanted to see where you lived. Now, give me the tour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God! (She frantically tries to clean up the bedroom as Richard starts the tour.)

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Ah well, this is the living room.

lines.speaker: Richard's Date
lines.text: Impressive.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: All right. This is the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Richard's Date
lines.text: Oh, that's real pretty. Wait a minute, don't I get to see the bedroom?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: The bedroom. Well it's pretty much your typical... (opens the door as Monica hides under the covers, and quickly closes the door before his date can see the room.) bedroom.

lines.speaker: Richard's Date
lines.text: We're still on this side of the door.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Richard's Date
lines.text: Yeah, but I didn't get to see it.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Oh shoot! Maybe next time. (yawns) Thanks for a lovely evening. (shows her out)
[Cut to into the bedroom, with Monica still hiding under the covers. Richard enters and sits down next to her.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (still hiding under the blankets) So um, who was she?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Oh, (laughs) that was the blind date that I told you about, she called and switched it to today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (still hiding under the blankets) Did you like her? And I'm just asking as a friend, because I am totally fine with this.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Well, you seem fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gets up) Okay, y'know what, I'm not fine, I'm not. I mean how can I be fine, hearing you come in with her, she wants to see your bedroom.... (pause) Y'know what, what if we're friends who don't see other people?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You mean like exclusive friends?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?! I mean this has been the most amazing week. Would it be so terrible? Even if we were friends who lived together. Or, maybe someday friends who stood up in front of their other friends, and vowed to be friends forever.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Wow. Y'know we're back where we were. Honey, I would love to do all that, but nothing's changed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not true, you don't have a moustache.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Okay, okay, one thing's changed. But we still want different things and we know how this is gonna end.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, I've got to walk out of here right now, 'cause getting over you is the hardest thing that I have ever had to do. I don't think I could do it again.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: I know I couldn't. So....
Monica kisses him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How 'bout one last game of racquetball?
They both kiss, and Richard picks her up and goes over to the bed and starts to lie down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Watch the thorns!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: (lying down) Ow!!
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Joey is entering, and he's very dejected.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Beth is really, really sick.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awwww.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Jo's there, but I don't think there's anything she could do.
Rachel hugs him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you want to put the book in the freezer?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (nodding his head) Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
Joey hands her the book and she puts it in the freezer.
End
